hypothesis: If pre-trained equivariant ML models (eqV2 or eSEN) are used as DFT surrogates to generate and initialize SSNEB pathways for solid-state phase transformations, then subsequent DFT-refined NEB calculations will recover DFT-quality minimum energy pathways, transition states, and barriers with significantly fewer iterations and reduced compute time than VASP-only NEB, because the ML models provide sufficiently accurate energies, forces, and stresses to start near the true MEP.
